parsedOutput.topic: Development and deployment of a WhatsApp Chat Summarizer for a hackathon
keyDecisions: Team name: StackCraft
Solution: AI summarizer with reply drafter for WhatsApp chat overload using React, Node.js, GPT-4o-mini
Wireframes to be implemented in Phase 2
Parser to support iOS 12-hour AM/PM, iOS 12-hour individual, and Android 24-hour timestamp formats, normalizing to ISO 8601
Multi-line messages handled by appending continuation lines
Prompt design to ignore system messages and media placeholders
Implement token counter for cost logging, storing data in a Supabase costs table
LLM integration to include retry with exponential backoff for timeouts and return 503 if both primary and fallback models fail
Frontend upload component to use react-dropzone, restrict to .txt files, max 5MB, and disable the upload button with a spinner during processing
Summary display page to use collapsible sections: Summary, Topics, Decisions, Action Items
Session history page to include a delete button per session
Summary page to redirect to login upon session expiration
Error state for invalid file type to have a 3-second minimum display duration during demo
actionItems: Tawhidul to add the deployment plan
Alice to review the parser Pull Request (PR) by EOD
Tawhidul to implement prompt design template
Tawhidul to add token counter as middleware on the summarize route
Tawhidul to implement LLM integration changes (retry, Supabase cost logging, 503 fallback)
Alice to add 'Max 5 MB .txt file' note to the upload component
Tawhidul to work on the session history API
Tawhidul to add a delete button to the session history page
Tawhidul to fix the auth guard on the summary page route
Alice to deploy the application to production
Tawhidul to conduct demo dry runs
notableFacts: The team name is StackCraft and the project aims to solve WhatsApp chat overload with an AI summarizer and reply drafter.
The tech stack includes React, Node.js, and GPT-4o-mini.
The parser component was completed, handling various timestamp formats and multi-line messages, and its PR was merged.
A prompt design template was drafted and refined, and testing with GPT-4o-mini showed accurate summarization, low latency, and low cost.
LLM integration, including cost logging to Supabase, retry mechanisms, and error handling, was completed and merged.
The frontend upload component was built using react-dropzone, restricting to .txt files up to 5MB, with appropriate error states and UI feedback.
The summary display page with collapsible sections and the session history page with delete functionality were implemented.
All unit, integration, and E2E tests passed, and the application was successfully deployed to production.
A demo script was prepared, and dry runs were conducted, leading to a minor UI improvement for error message display duration.
parsedOutput.summaryText: Alice and Tawhidul collaborated on developing a WhatsApp chat summarizer for a hackathon, covering backend parsing, AI integration with GPT-4o-mini, and frontend UI for upload, summary display, and session history. They successfully completed development, testing, and production deployment, finalizing a demo script with dry runs for presentation.

parsedOutput.topic: Development of a WhatsApp chat summarizer application (Sprint 1 completion & Sprint 2 initiation)
keyDecisions: First sprint planning meeting rescheduled from 9 AM to 10 AM.
Dave was assigned to handle QA for Sprint 1.
Group name changed to 'Dev Team Q2 - Sprint 1' and later 'Dev Team Q2 - Sprint 1 Final' for context.
API schema updated to include a 'status' field and 'created_at'/'completed_at' timestamps.
GPT-4o-mini selected as the primary LLM, with Claude Haiku as fallback.
Prompt template refined to ignore system messages/media and cap output length at 300 tokens.
Parser fixes implemented for multi-line messages with blank lines and sender names containing colons.
Sprint 1 goals (file upload, parser, LLM summarization, API schema, tests) were completed.
Sprint 2 scope defined: frontend UI, authentication, session history, error handling, and production deployment.
Summary page timestamps decided: relative time for display, ISO 8601 in the database.
Parser updated to normalize Windows-style CRLF line endings to LF.
E2E test plan updated to include a scenario for session expiry mid-upload.
actionItems: Alice: Update sprint planning invite, send updated invite, draft API schema, work on LLM comparison, work on API docs and frontend scaffold, test summarize endpoint, lead sprint reviews/planning, review E2E plan, review frontend upload PR, review auth integration PR.

Bob: Share draft agenda, send edge-case fixture, work on upload endpoint and MIME validation, work on LLM integration endpoint and prompt template, fix sender name regex, handle backend auth integration, add CRLF normalization to parser.
Carol: Write Jest tests for parser, fix multi-line blank line issue, write integration tests for summarize flow, take frontend upload flow, document LLM decision.

Dave: Start test plan, run smoke tests, work on end-to-end upload flow test, deploy upload endpoint, run regression suite, run automated summarize endpoint tests, write e2e test plan, add session expiry scenario.
notableFacts: The project team consists of Alice (lead), Bob (developer), Carol (developer), and Dave (QA).
A draft agenda for sprint planning was shared and approved.
Staging environment was temporarily down for maintenance.
The parser component was developed, reviewed, and merged, including fixes for specific edge cases.
An API schema draft was created and refined based on team feedback.
The upload endpoint was deployed to staging and passed initial smoke tests.
LLM comparison results were shared, informing the choice of GPT-4o-mini.
Two critical parser bugs were identified via regression tests and promptly fixed.
The summarize endpoint was deployed to staging and manually tested with various chat exports.
Sprint 1 successfully delivered core features including file upload, WhatsApp parser, LLM summarization, and API schema.
Sprint 2 commenced with initial progress on frontend upload component and backend authentication integration.
parsedOutput.summaryText: The Dev Team Q2 successfully completed Sprint 1, delivering a WhatsApp chat summarizer with file upload, parsing for iOS/Android formats, an LLM summarization endpoint using GPT-4o-mini, and a defined API schema. They also fixed critical parser bugs and conducted thorough testing. Sprint 2 has commenced, focusing on frontend UI, authentication, session management, and preparing for production deployment, with initial progress made on key components.
